segments.text: This is how wealth per person changed across the world, from 1960 to 2024.
At the start, United States leads, with New Zealand and Canada right behind.
By 1971, Monaco moves into first place, pushing United States off the top spot.
By 1981, Brunei Darussalam moves into first place, pushing Monaco off the top spot.
By 1992, Monaco moves into first place, pushing Brunei Darussalam off the top spot.
In 2003, the front of the table is Monaco, Liechtenstein, and Bermuda.
In 2013, the front of the table is Monaco, Liechtenstein, and Luxembourg.
By 2024, the leading names are Monaco, Bermuda, Luxembourg.
The big story here is the difference between large economies and truly rich societies.
Follow Datavault for more animated data stories driven by real numbers.
